commit.message:
Move menu bar to top of window matching System 7 screen layout

In real System 7, the menu bar is the topmost element on screen —
not below a title bar. This commit:

- Removed System7TitleBar from MainWindow (kept for dialog windows)
- Menu bar is now the first element, doubling as the window drag handle
- Outer border has CornerRadius=8,8,0,0 for rounded top corners matching
  the classic Mac screen bezel look
- Menu bar border uses CornerRadius=7,7,0,0 to follow the outer shape
- Window uses Background=Transparent with TransparencyLevelHint for
  rounded corners to render correctly (no square artifacts)
- Added comprehensive Fluent theme overrides in App.axaml:
  - All MenuItem states (normal, pointerover, pressed, open) explicitly
    set to white bg + black text or black bg + white text
  - MenuFlyoutPresenter template Border override for white bg
  - Chicago font propagated to all menu items and flyout presenters
  - Font size set to 14 across all menu elements
- Menu bar border is wired for drag via PointerPressed in code-behind
- Added Alt+F4 handler since OS chrome close button is removed
- ChromeHeight reduced from 47 to 30 (no title bar, just menu bar)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace OS title bar and status bar with authentic System 7 chrome

- Replaced Windows title bar with System7TitleBar on MainWindow
  (SystemDecorations=None, custom striped title bar with close box and drag)
- Removed status bar and help button — all functions accessible via menus
- Fixed menu bar text: added explicit Foreground=#000000 styles in App.axaml
  to override Fluent theme defaults that caused white-on-white text
- Fixed dropdown menus: white background, black text, black highlight with
  white text on hover — matching System 7 menu behavior
- Removed underscore mnemonics from menu headers for cleaner Mac appearance
- Replaced Apple logo char (F8FF) with ⌘ symbol for cross-platform rendering
- Updated ResizeToScale to account for new chrome height (title bar + menu)
  instead of old status bar + Windows chrome constants
- Card display now fills edge-to-edge with no margin (Margin=0)
- Background changed from #333333 to #C0C0C0 (System 7 gray)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor UI to System 7 aesthetic and fix placeholder floppy scaling

- Fixed floppy disk scale formula from 3*min(w,h)/400 to min(w,h)/160
  for smoother proportional growth across zoom levels (1.5-2x per zoom step)

- Fixed corner chamfer artifacts on top-right by zeroing transparent pixels
  at initialization instead of stamping them during render

- Styled menu bar with authentic System 7 look: white background,
  Chicago/Geneva font family, proper spacing and padding. Added File/View/Help
  menu structure matching original HyperCard UI conventions.

- Simplified status bar: removed help button, keep minimal status text only
  (no longer needed with menu bar help access)

- Restyled StackPickerWindow dialog for authentic System 7 appearance:
  - #C0C0C0 background (System gray), proper borders and corners
  - Removed monospace font, now uses Geneva/Helvetica
  - ListBoxes render with white bg and proper System 7 styling
  - Default button wrapping with bold font for visual prominence
  - CenterOwner positioning for proper modal behavior

- Updated StackViewModel default status text to empty string

- Applied ListBoxItem style in App.axaml with proper text color and font,
  fixing list selection contrast (black text on highlight background)

commit.message:
fix: treat Geant4 kInfinity as no-intersection in TraceRay (#255)

Geant4's kInfinity (CLHEP) is 9.9999e+99, a large but finite double.
std::isinf() returns false for it, so rays where DistanceToIn returns
kInfinity (no intersection) were being treated as valid hits.

This caused SurfaceNormal to be evaluated at kInfinity * direction,
a nonsensical point far outside the solid, producing garbage normals
and false surface-normal mismatches.  For G4TwistedTubs (dphi=120°),
this accounted for all 1381 reported surface-normal failures.

Fix:
- DistanceString: also print "kInfinity" for values >= kInfinity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct G4ScaledSolid exit normals in DistanceToOut fixture (#253)

* fix: correct G4ScaledSolid exit normals in DistanceToOut fixture

G4ScaledSolid::DistanceToOut (Geant4 bug, present through at least 11.4.1)
calls fScale->TransformNormal() on the exit normal returned by the unscaled
base solid.  TransformNormal maps normals from global→local frame, but the
base-solid normal (solNorm) is already in the local (unscaled) frame.  The
correct transform is InverseTransformNormal() (local→global), which applies
the inverse-transpose of the scale matrix (divide each component by its
scale factor).  G4ScaledSolid::SurfaceNormal() uses InverseTransformNormal()
correctly, explaining why SurfaceNormal yields 0 mismatches while
DistanceToOut exit normals show 2039/2048 failures for the non-uniformly
scaled sphere fixture (semi-axes 15×20×5 mm).

Fix in G4OCCT: introduce G4ScaledSolidFixed, a minimal subclass that
overrides DistanceToOut to recompute the exit normal via SurfaceNormal()
at the exit point.  SurfaceNormal() is correctly implemented in Geant4,
so this gives the geometrically correct outward normal without any
modification to the Geant4 source tree.  The fixture builder now constructs
G4ScaledSolidFixed instead of G4ScaledSolid for native-solid comparisons.

Remove the broad G4ScaledSolid xfail in fixture_validation.cc (reason was
stale: 'not implemented yet').  Add a comment explaining the Geant4 bug and
the workaround.  Document the bug and fix strategy in provenance.yaml.

commit.message:
feat: reduce FN 25→18, F1 87.9%→91.6% (#297)

* Fix CWE-676/918 scoring and add SSRF/VLA detection patterns

Reduce false negatives from 25 to 18 on fixtures benchmark (F1: 87.9% → 91.6%).

Scoring fixes:
- Add CWE-676 to BufferOverflow, FormatString, InsecureTempFile semantic
  class CWE lists so gets/strcpy/sprintf/mktemp findings also match CWE-676
- Add CWE-918 (SSRF) to injection category_to_cwes fallback mapping

New patterns:
- Python: urlopen/urllib.request.urlopen for SSRF detection (CWE-918)
- C/C++: VLA detection (char buf[var]) for stack overflow (CWE-119/787)
- C/C++: alloca with variable size for stack overflow (CWE-119/787)

Results: P=100%, R=84.5% (was 78.4%), F1=91.6% (was 87.9%), FN=18 (was 25)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add gets, strcpy, strcat, sprintf, vsprintf, mktemp, tmpnam, tempnam
to is_unsafe_api_usage() function list for CWE-676 classification.
Add CWE-918 to injection family mapping in cwe_family() for SSRF scoring.

Recovers 4 false negatives (3 CWE-676 + 1 CWE-918), reducing FN from 18 to 14.

Scale placeholder floppy disk proportionally with zoom level

Previously the disk icon stayed at a fixed scale=1 (32×32px rendered)
for all zoom levels from 1× through 2×, only growing at 4×.

Changed the scale formula from `Math.Max(1, Math.Min(w,h) / 512)` to
`Math.Max(3, 3 * Math.Min(w,h) / 400)`, anchoring the baseline at
scale=3 (96×96px) at 1× zoom and growing proportionally with the window
so the floppy and its blinking '?' scale correctly with Ctrl+1/2/3/4.

All changes must meet WCAG 2.2 AA at minimum. - Prefer semantic HTML over ARIA where possible. - Use WAI-ARIA only when native semantics are insufficient. ### Minimal changes - Apply the smallest change that fully resolves the accessibility issue. - Avoid unnecessary refactoring. - Preserve existing behavior, layout, and intent unless the accessibility fix requires a change. ### Deterministic behavior - Apply deterministic fixes: the same code pattern should produce the same result. - Avoid subjective or stylistic changes unless they are directly tied to accessibility. ## Accessibility rules - Prefer existing visible text for accessible names whenever possible. - Reuse nearby labels, headings, button text, or other visible copy before introducing hidden text such as `aria-label`. - Do not remove existing accessibility attributes such as `title`, `alt`, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby` unless they are being replaced with a stronger or more correct alternative. - Prefer relationships to visible content, such as `aria-labelledby`, when that produces a clearer and more maintainable result. ## i18n rules - Any new or replacement user-facing string, or any new or replacement string exposed to assistive technology as an accessible name, must use `i18n.translate('message.id', { defaultMessage: 'English fallback' })`. - Do not introduce that copy as a raw string literal. - If the file already uses a shared i18n object such as `i18nTexts.modalTitle`, keep that pattern for new strings in that file. - Do not localize programmatic identifiers that are not user-visible, such as radio group `name` values. - commit.message:
[Foundation] Add KeepHeadersAfterDecompression app context switch to NSUrlSessionHandler (#24957)

Add an opt-out switch 'Foundation.NSUrlSessionHandler.KeepHeadersAfterDecompression'
that, when enabled, preserves the original Content-Encoding and Content-Length headers
on auto-decompressed responses instead of removing them.

This allows apps that depend on the original headers to restore the previous behavior.

fix: correct G4DisplacedSolid fixture STEP translation (#249)

* fix: correct G4DisplacedSolid fixture STEP translation

G4DisplacedSolid(base, nullptr, translation) places the *center* of the
base solid at the displacement vector.  G4Box(halfX, halfY, halfZ) is
centered at the origin, so the displaced solid spans:
  [tx-halfX, tx+halfX] x [ty-halfY, ty+halfY] x [tz-halfZ, tz+halfZ]
= [2, 22] x [-22, 8] x [5, 45]  for translation (12,-7,25) and box (20,30,40).

In DRAWEXE, `box` creates a box with one CORNER at the origin, so the
previous generate.tcl `ttranslate displaced_box 12 -7 25` placed the
CORNER at (12,-7,25), producing a solid spanning (12,-7,25)→(32,23,65)
with center at (22,8,45) — displaced by exactly (halfX, halfY, halfZ) =
(10,15,20) from the Geant4 solid center (12,-7,25).

Fix: translate by (tx-halfX, ty-halfY, tz-halfZ) = (2,-22,5) so the
center of the STEP box aligns with the Geant4 displaced solid center.

Regenerate shape.step; volume is unchanged at 20*30*40 = 24000 mm³.

commit.message:
Fix MIDI routing: icon zone clicks don't steal keyboard focus

Clicking a clip's play/stop/record icon (icon zone) now only triggers
the action without changing the selected track. Previously, every
session grid click changed m_selectedTrack and the virtual keyboard
target, so launching audio clips redirected MIDI input away from the
instrument track, causing the virtual keyboard to stop producing sound.

Now only content area clicks change track selection, matching
Ableton's behavior where launch buttons are fire-and-forget.

fix: move modal to body level to fix stacking context blocking modal

CSS animation on .page-content (animation: fadeIn) creates a new stacking
context. Bootstrap backdrop (z-index 1050) is appended to <body>, outside
this context, making it appear ON TOP of the modal content — screen goes grey
and nothing is clickable.

Fix: add {% block modals %} to base.html placed directly under <body>,
before Bootstrap JS. Move #itemModal from {% block content %} (inside <main>)
to {% block modals %} so it is a direct child of <body> with no stacking
context ancestors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update CMakeLists.txt

fix: pre-fetch compiler.wasm to bypass Emscripten internal fetch

Emscripten's WASM loading fails with 'both async and sync fetching
of the wasm failed' when the page is opened via file:// (Chrome
blocks cross-origin fetch/XHR for file: origins) or when internal
scriptDirectory path resolution goes wrong.

Fix: compiler-api.js now fetches compiler.wasm explicitly and passes
the buffer as Module.wasmBinary. Emscripten skips its internal fetch
entirely when wasmBinary is pre-set. The catch() also surfaces a clear
error message telling the user to serve via HTTP with make serve.

Added a serve Makefile target: cd $(DEMO) && python3 -m http.server 8080

fix: preserve colour grading in table output (#122)

tabulate 0.10.0 strips ANSI colour codes from numeric cell values
during its number-parsing phase, leaving only the bold attribute.
This caused Files, Commits, Comments, and Age columns to appear as
plain white bold text instead of their graded colours.

- Pass disable_numparse=True to tabulate() so ANSI-styled numeric
  values are treated as strings and colour codes are preserved
- Pass color=_stdout_is_tty() to click.echo() so ANSI codes survive
  in TTY contexts (previously used auto-detect which could strip them)
- Add _compress_styled() helper to preserve colour when _auto_fit
  compresses Checks and Approved columns on narrow terminals
- Add colour styling to format_mergeable_status() via click.style(),
  consistent with format_check_status() and format_approval_status()

Closes #121

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: extract DEB build to build-deb.sh to fix CI packaging failure (#17)

* fix: extract DEB build to build-deb.sh to fix CI packaging failure

The `build-deb` CI job was producing no .deb file because the
`bash -c "..."` argument in the step contained a comment with
unescaped double quotes (`"trying to overwrite"`), which caused
the outer shell to prematurely close the quoted string. This made
the pip install and dpkg-deb steps unreachable.

Fix: move the full DEB build logic into `build-deb.sh` (a proper
shell script with `set -euo pipefail`) and update the CI step to
call it directly. Also add `make deb` Makefile target.

Co-authored-by: lobis <35803280+lobis@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/lobis/gfal/sessions/cd6c696a-1ec2-4a6c-8e35-933fce60ddb1

* chore: upgrade DEB build from ubuntu:22.04 to ubuntu:24.04

Co-authored-by: lobis <35803280+lobis@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/lobis/gfal/sessions/cd6c696a-1ec2-4a6c-8e35-933fce60ddb1

* fix: add --break-system-packages to pip calls for Ubuntu 24.04 PEP 668

Ubuntu 24.04 enforces PEP 668 and rejects pip installs to the system
Python without --break-system-packages. Since we run inside a
purpose-built Docker container this is safe.

Co-authored-by: lobis <35803280+lobis@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/lobis/gfal/sessions/f9b49c4b-8cf8-4495-b9f0-236cb183f862

* fix: prune only 'rich' package exactly, not 'rich_click' in build-deb.sh

The glob `rich*` was removing `rich_click` (bundled dependency) along
with the `rich` system package. Changed to explicit path removal.
Same fix for `click*` → explicit paths to avoid future issues.
